headline: Survey at 31% completion; strong early engagement but scale readiness concerning.
collection_status: 424 responses collected against 1,350 target (31% progress). Regional coverage uneven: Lindi and Mara dominate (37% of responses) while Rukwa, Singida, and Songwe underrepresented. Pace suggests 2-3 weeks needed to reach target at current rate.
key_findings: Teacher cohort (KF lower, upper, non-KF) represents 58% of responses; head teachers 23%—good school-level penetration but limited district/regional official input (only 6% combined).
Expansion priorities crystal clear: 153 favour expand_kf, 98 new_programs, 89 policy—80% consensus on growth over strengthening (70 votes).
Critical gap: scale readiness 2.7/5.0 vs partner satisfaction 4.4/5.0—stakeholders satisfied but perceive low institutional readiness for expansion.
Qualitative feedback emphasizes teacher incentives, government policy integration, and digital monitoring—expansion without teacher support will stall.
flags: Scale readiness 2.7/5.0 is red: 47% response gap and low readiness suggest expansion push will face implementation barriers if not addressed.
Regional imbalance critical: Rukwa (12), Singida (25), Songwe (29) severely underrepresented; risk of biased findings and weak regional buy-in.
Official district/regional engagement weak (12 responses total); expansion requires policy champions—urgently need DED, RED, and arc engagement.
recommendation: Immediately launch targeted outreach to underrepresented regions (Rukwa, Singida, Songwe) and priority recruitment of district/regional officials (DED, RED, ARC) to reach 60% coverage within 10 days. Parallel action: conduct rapid readiness diagnostic with 20-30 head teachers and officials to unblock the 2.7 scale readiness gap before finalizing expansion strategy.
